danger_signals.visual_form: Goombas, Koopas, Cheep-Cheeps rendered on screen in first-person perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring player response via shooting

danger_signals.visual_form: Level transition to boss arena; Koopalings piloting giant mechs or enlarged enemy versions appear
danger_signals.meaning: High-difficulty threat requiring focused firepower and strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar visual indicator shown on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player proximity to life loss; increased vulnerability state
Temporal threat; failure to complete level within time limit causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental bottomless gaps visible in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death risk if player falls with fewer than three coins

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon power meter on HUD dropping during rapid fire
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical vulnerability; extended pause between shots increases enemy attack window

safety_signals.visual_form: Mushrooms, flowers, and stars rendered as collectible items; visual feedback when acquired
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary stat enhancements: extra health, firepower boost, or invincibility frames

safety_signals.visual_form: Question mark blocks shot to release coins; 60-coin threshold visualization
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation toward extra life; safety buffer against game over

safety_signals.visual_form: First-person perspective anchored to Yoshi mount; visible in level transitions
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency state; controlled mobility and combat platform

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mario and Yoshi as protagonists; Mario's iconic silhouette in first-person POV
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jewelry Land setting; visual separation between allied and enemy-controlled zones through level design

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bowser and Koopalings as identifiable boss entities; mech designs piloted by Koopalings (Iggy's invention)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard Goombas, Koopas, Cheep-Cheeps as foot soldiers; enlarged versions as mini-bosses; mechs as faction-specific technology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jewelry Land territories corrupted by invasion; split kingdom geography indicates occupation zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: King Fret and Prince Pine mentioned as kidnapped objectives
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Targets of rescue mission; their imprisonment reflects kingdom vulnerability state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protected kingdom with 12 magic gems intact; unified geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemy presence; normal level progression expected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kingdom split in two by earthquake; visual destruction and corruption; gems scattered across 12 levels as mission objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bowser's forces occupy territories; Koopalings and mechs deployed; enemy patrols and boss encounters in each level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person perspective of level environment with enemies and hazards visible; branching path architecture visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively attack; time limit actively counts down; power gauge responds to player firing rate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Elevated visual presentation of boss entity; Koopaling in mech or Bowser at level terminus
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss-specific attack patterns; enhanced enemy AI; concentrated damage requirement for progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gem recovery visual; overworld map update showing level completion state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player advances to next level; score registered on leaderboard; difficulty increase unlocks with cheat code after game completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Earthquake caused by gem theft visually manifests as kingdom split in two; this geographic division communicates the magnitude of invasion and justifies multi-level quest structure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type placement as faction signature
environmental_storytelling.example: Koopalings piloting giant mechs designed by Iggy serves as visual technology signature of Bowser's faction; upgraded enemy variants (larger Goombas/Koopas) indicate military escalation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Level-end boss progression as narrative escalation
environmental_storytelling.example: Each level culminates in confrontation with Koopaling or Bowser; visual elevation of boss arena signals story progression toward final confrontation
12 magic gems scattered across 12 levels represent kingdom's life force; visual collection of gems (shown on HUD or in cutscenes) communicates restoration of world stability
First-person perspective using Mode 7 graphics creates visual illusion of depth and 3D space; this communicates player immersion into invaded territory rather than observing from distance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible on screen requiring targeting and shooting; behavior changes based on level branching path selection
Elevated attack complexity; giant mech form requires sustained firepower; pattern-based attack behavior distinct from standard enemies
Appears at game finale; represents faction leader; requires maximum firepower and invincibility stars for effective damage; ultimate threat manifestation
Stationary; becomes active when shot by player; provides temporary buff (health, firepower, invincibility)
Continuous forward motion through level; controlled shooting via light gun aiming; vulnerable to enemy fire and environmental hazards; health bar decreases with damage

notes: Yoshi's Safari uses first-person perspective with light gun mechanics to create unique danger/safety signal vocabulary compared to traditional Mario games. Visual communication relies heavily on: (1) enemy placement and scale variation to indicate threat level; (2) environmental geometry (pits, paths) to communicate hazard zones; (3) HUD elements (health bar, power gauge, timer, coin counter) for state feedback; (4) boss arena visual elevation to mark narrative progression; (5) Mode 7 perspective effect to immerse player in invaded territory. Faction identity is communicated through character design (Mario/Yoshi vs. Bowser/Koopalings) and technology signature (mechs as Bowser faction innovation). World state transitions from stable to compromised kingdom are communicated through narrative setup (gem theft, kingdom split) and level progression structure (12 levels = 12 gems = restoration path). Limited sources restrict depth of analysis on subtle visual communication techniques not explicitly documented.
